skill.name: higgsfield-product-photoshoot
skill.description: Generate brand-quality product images through Higgsfield product-photoshoot prompt enhancement on GPT Image 2 / gpt_image_2. Entry point for professional brand/product visuals. Supports 10 modes: product_shot, lifestyle_scene, closeup_product_with_person, moodboard_pin, hero_banner, social_carousel, ad_creative_pack, virtual_model_tryout, conceptual_product, restyle.
skill.summary: AI-powered product photography skill that creates studio-quality brand images, lifestyle scenes, and ad creatives through prompt enhancement.
skill.author: higgsfield-ai

security_audit.summary: All 97 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is a markdown documentation file containing CLI command examples in fenced code blocks, not programmatic shell execution. The static analyzer incorrectly interpreted markdown backtick-fenced bash examples as Ruby-style shell backtick injection. The curl-to-sh install pattern on line 35 is documentation for manual user installation, not automated execution. Hardcoded URLs and path traversal detections are placeholder examples in documentation. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious patterns were detected.

medium_findings.title: External Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md contains bash command examples in fenced code blocks (```bash). The static analyzer flagged these as shell backtick execution, but they are documentation for manual user execution. The skill only programmatically runs 'higgsfield product-photoshoot create' via the CLI tool. All 76 flagged locations are markdown code fences, not code injection.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: File content confirms all flagged locations are markdown fenced code blocks showing CLI usage examples - no programmatic shell execution exists.

low_findings.title: Curl-to-Shell Install Pattern Documented
low_findings.description: Line 35 documents 'curl ... | sh' as a manual install step for the higgsfield CLI tool. This is standard practice for CLI tool installation documentation. The skill does not execute this command programmatically; it is presented for the user to run manually.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install command is inside a markdown fenced code block clearly labeled as bootstrap instructions, not executed by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Example URLs
low_findings.description: Lines 197-199 contain placeholder example URLs (cdn.higgsfield.ai) showing the expected output format. These are fictitious documentation examples, not actual API endpoints being called.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs use placeholder paths with '...' clearly indicating they are format examples, and they appear in a 'Delivering results' documentation section showing output formatting.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Placeholder in Example URLs
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged '...' in example URLs as path traversal sequences. These are text placeholders in documentation examples (e.g., '/.../job_abc.jpg'), not actual directory traversal patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The '...' appears inside example output URLs in a 'Delivering results' section - clearly a placeholder ellipsis, not the '../' directory traversal operator.

content.user_title: Generate Brand-Quality Product Photos with AI
content.value_statement: Professional product photography requires expensive studios, equipment, and photographers. This skill creates brand-quality product images, lifestyle scenes, and marketing creatives through AI-powered prompt enhancement using the higgsfield CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate studio-quality product shots on neutral or branded backgrounds
Create lifestyle scenes showing products in real-world environments
Produce coordinated ad creative packs for social media platforms
Generate moodboard-style Pinterest pins and hero banners for campaigns
Restyle existing product images with new aesthetics or seasonal themes
Create virtual model try-ons for fashion and accessories
limitations: Cannot generate images without a product photo or detailed product description
Does not support video generation or avatar-based content
Cannot write or modify the underlying GPT Image 2 prompt directly
Requires the higgsfield CLI to be installed and authenticated on the system

use_cases.title: Ecommerce Product Photography
use_cases.description: An online store owner needs consistent, professional product images for their Shopify or Etsy catalog without hiring a photographer.
use_cases.target_user: Ecommerce seller or small business owner

use_cases.title: Social Media Ad Creatives
use_cases.description: A social media manager needs a coordinated pack of ad variants for a Meta or TikTok campaign in different formats.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professional or social media manager

use_cases.title: Seasonal Brand Refresh
use_cases.description: A brand manager wants to restyle existing product images for a holiday campaign with a new aesthetic or seasonal theme.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Product Shot
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a clean studio product shot of my moisturizer on a white background. Mode: product_shot.
prompt_templates.scenario: User uploads a product photo and wants a basic catalog-style image.

prompt_templates.title: Lifestyle Scene for Social Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Make 3 lifestyle scenes of my candle brand. Show the candles on a cozy kitchen counter with soft natural light. Mode: lifestyle_scene.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants social-media-ready lifestyle images for Instagram or website.

prompt_templates.title: Ad Creative Pack for Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ad creative pack for my protein bar. Generate 5 variants for Meta ads with different backgrounds and angles. Mode: ad_creative_pack.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs multiple ad variations for a paid social campaign.

prompt_templates.title: Restyle for Seasonal Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Restyle my existing product image. Apply a Christmas theme with quiet-luxury aesthetic. Preserve the product appearance. Mode: restyle.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has existing product images and wants to adapt them for a seasonal campaign.

output_examples.input: Create a clean studio lifestyle scene of my ceramic coffee mug on a wooden table with morning light.
Make a moodboard pin for my candle brand with cottagecore aesthetic.
Restyle my existing product photos for a Valentine's campaign with romantic aesthetic.

best_practices: Always upload a reference product photo for the best quality and most accurate results
Be specific about the intended platform or use case when selecting a mode
Use the restyle mode when you want to change the aesthetic of an existing image without altering the product
anti_patterns: Do not write the gpt_image_2 prompt directly - the backend handles prompt assembly for better quality
Do not use higgsfield generate create gpt_image_2 instead of product-photoshoot create - this bypasses the prompt enhancer
Do not ask more than 4 questions before submitting a generation request to keep the conversation concise

faq.question: What is the higgsfield-product-photoshoot skill?
faq.answer: It generates brand-quality product images through AI-powered prompt enhancement using the higgsfield CLI.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the higgsfield CLI installed and authenticated via higgsfield auth login.

faq.question: What modes are available for product photography?
faq.answer: There are 10 modes: product_shot, lifestyle_scene, closeup_product_with_person, moodboard_pin, hero_banner, social_carousel, ad_creative_pack, virtual_model_tryout, conceptual_product, and restyle.

faq.question: Can I use my own product photos as reference images?
faq.answer: Yes, you can upload product photos as reference images. The skill accepts local file paths or upload IDs.

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and higgsfield-generate?
faq.answer: This skill is specialized for product photography with a backend prompt enhancer. Use higgsfield-generate for text-to-image, video, and avatar workflows.

faq.question: How many images can I generate in one request?
faq.answer: You can generate 1 to 10 images per request using the count option. For carousel and ad packs, the count determines the number of slides or variants.
